title: CTAB–Chloroform DNA Extraction from Ethanol-Preserved Freshwater Phytobentos
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7hq9r","text":"This protocol describes a CTAB-based extraction of eDNA from particulate material pelleted from an ethanol-preserved river/stream water sample. It uses a single chloroform extraction, isopropanol precipitation, two 70% ethanol washes, and elution in Qiagen EB buffer. The protocol is adapted from Strand et al. (2019) with modifications for a pellet-based workflow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8rj35","text":"Contamination prevention (recommended): Use filter tips, wear gloves, change gloves frequently, and decontaminate work surfaces with a bleach-based solution before and during the work.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4d2m8","text":"Pellet handling: DNA pellets may be small and can be nearly invisible to the naked eye. Use extra care when removing the isopropanol supernatant after precipitation and during the 70% ethanol wash steps to avoid pellet loss.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qcvs","text":"Optional clarification step: If the recovered aqueous phase remains visibly turbid after chloroform separation, an additional chloroform extraction can be performed to improve phase clarity before precipitation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6bcr7","text":"Pre-cool isopropanol and 70% ethanol at −20 °C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"84sf2","text":"Preheat a heat block, water bath, or a temperature-controlled incubator (suitable for microcentrifuge tubes) to 65 °C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6435u","text":"Decontaminate the work surface and equipment and organise a clean workflow to minimise contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fvgue","text":"Chloroform is toxic and volatile: handle in a fume hood; dispose of organic waste properly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dctkl","text":"Ethanol and isopropanol are flammable: keep away from ignition sources.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aa4j5","text":"Bleach-based disinfectants are irritants/corrosive: wear gloves and avoid mixing with acids.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

mM","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fq0a","text":"Tris-HCl, 20 mM EDTA, 1.4 M NaCl, 2% (w/v) CTAB (no mg/mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9vfie","text":"Chloroform, pure (p.a.)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"921i","text":"Isopropanol (p.a.)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8sg74","text":"70% ethanol  (prepared from p.a. ethanol + autoclaved deionized water)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2c3k7","text":"Qiagen EB buffer (10 mM Tris-Cl, pH ~8.5)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"75nti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85la8","text":"Consumables","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d2dcv","text":"2.0 mL microcentrifuge tubes (standard snap-cap “Eppendorf” type)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rs1h","text":"Filter tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74fmc","text":"Gloves","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"59tn6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"foin","text":"Equipment","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"u0el","text":"Microcentrifuge for 2.0 mL tubes (able to run at ~13,500 rpm / max speed)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1f0gv","text":"Temperature-controlled device (heat block, water bath, or incubator) capable of 65 °C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egk81","text":"Vortex mixer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b0bif","text":"Vacuum concentrator (optional; alternative is heat block drying)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Resuspend particulates in the ethanol-preserved sample container by manual shaking until homogenized.
Transfer 2 mL of homogenized ethanol mixture into a 2.0 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 20 minutes (max speed).
Dry the pellet for 00:10:00 using either a vacuum concentrator or a heat block (open cap), ensuring ethanol is removed.
Carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Add 500 µL CTAB buffer (room temperature) to the dried pellet.
Vortex continuously for 00:15:00 .
Freeze at -20 °C until fully frozen (typically 01:00:00 ); samples may be stored at -20 °C Overnight and processed the next day.
Thaw at 65 °C for 00:15:00 .
Add 4 µL 20 µL Proteinase K ( 20 mg/mL ) and vortex briefly.
Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00 .
Add 400 µL chloroform and mix gently with the pipette tip.
Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 15 minutes .
Carefully transfer ~ 300 µL of the upper aqueous phase to a new 2.0 or 1,5mL tube using a fresh filter tip, avoiding the interphase.
Add 400 µL ice-cold isopropanol (stored at -20 °C ) and mix by inverting the tube several times.
Incubate 00:15:00 at 4 °C .
Centrifuge for 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 15 minutes .
Remove supernatant carefully without losing the pellet.
Add 500 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol (stored at -20 °C ) and briefly vortex.
Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes .
Carefully remove supernatant.
Repeat etanol wash once more go to step #19 .
Dry the pellet (open cap) for ~ 00:10:00 in a vacuum centrifuge or on a 65 °C heat block (avoid airborne contamination).
Dissolve DNA pellet in 30 µL EB buffer (or sterile milliQ water), vortex and spin down. Let the DNA dissolve for at least 1 hour before further analysis (or store in fridge/freeze).
creator: Pavla Urbankova
